channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple says its iPhone Lockdown Mode has never been hacked by spyware — here's how to turn it on
item.description: Four years in, hackers and scammers haven't been able to get past Lockdown Mode, Apple says.

item.title: SteelSeries’ feature-packed Nova Pro Wireless headset is $80 off
item.description: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless came out almost four years ago, yet it’s still one of the best gaming headsets you can buy. Turns out, it’s not easy for other companies to duplicate all that SteelSeries got right with this one — mixing multiple audio sources, active noise cancellation, a retractable mic, hot-swappable batteries, […]

item.title: 'The complete package': I tested the LG G6 OLED TV side-by-side with the G5 in our testing rooms — and the difference is clear
item.description: The LG G5 was one of 2025's best OLED TVs — but the LG G6 improves in the right places, even more dramatically than I expected

item.title: 'One of the best smart appliances you can own right now' — the Tineco wet-dry vacuum transformed my cleaning routine, and it's 50% off at Amazon
item.description: The Tineco wet-dry vacuum is 'one of the best smart appliances you can own right now,' and Amazon has a massive 50% discount, bringing the price down to a record low.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Sunday, March 29 (game #1525)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Sunday, March 29 (game #1022)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Sunday, March 29 (game #756)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: ‘The Mummy 4’ Is Getting the Family Back Together
item.description: It ain't just Fraser and Weisz: John Hannah's also back in the franchise game with 'The Mummy 4.'

item.title: JD Vance Says UFOs Are Actually Demons
item.description: "I think that one of the Devil's great tricks is to convince people he never existed."

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
item.description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

item.title: Were tracking the best Apple deals in Amazons Spring Sale — new lows on AirPods, iPads, Apple Watches, and MacBooks
item.description: See all the best Apple deals from the Amazon Big Spring Sale, including $50 off AirPods Pro 3 and $250 off M4 MacBook Air laptops.

item.title: En promo, quelle est la meilleure affaire entre le nouveau Nothing Phone (4a) Pro et le Xiaomi Redmi Note 15 Pro+ ?
item.description: À moins de 450 euros, le milieu de gamme n’a jamais été aussi disputé. Entre le très original Nothing Phone (4a) Pro et le solide Xiaomi Redmi Note 15 Pro Plus 5G, deux smartphones notés 8/10 par Frandroid, le choix devient presque difficile, surtout quand les deux sont en promotion.

item.title: My favorite smart glasses are at record prices for the Amazon Spring Sale — prices start at $249
item.description: Are you an early adopter? Mashable-tested AR glasses from TCL and Xreal are on sale during the Amazon Big Spring Sale 2026.

item.title: Victim of Jeffrey Epstein files class-action lawsuit against Google
item.description: A new lawsuit alleges that Google's artificial intelligence improperly published private information of Jeffrey Epstein's victims.

item.title: TikTok’s policy for AI ads isn’t working
item.description: I've been struggling to tell whether the ads appearing in my TikTok feeds have been made with generative AI tools. As someone who spends a great deal of time scrutinizing images and videos for the usual "tells" that something was synthetically generated, some of the promotions I've seen have definitely sparked suspicion. For several weeks, […]

item.title: Meta’s legal defeat could be a victory for children, or a loss for everyone
item.description: Is social media not just bad, but illegally bad? Should tech companies pay for making it that way? According to two US juries - and no shortage of outside commentary - the answer to both questions is "yes." Earlier this week, two juries - one in New Mexico, one in Los Angeles - held Meta […]

item.title: 6 new 4K Blu-rays from March 2026 to add to your collection
item.description: For this month's Blu-ray Bounty, I'm checking out 6 discs released in March that would be great additions to any 4K collection.

item.title: NYT Pips hints, answers for March 28, 2026
item.description: The New York Times' latest game, Pips, brings domino fun to your desktop. How to play Pips as well as hints in case you get stuck.

item.title: NYT Connections Sports Edition today: Hints and answers for March 28, 2026
item.description: Connections: Sports Edition is a New York Times word game about finding common sports threads between words. How to solve the day's puzzle.
